description: Canonical single source of truth (SSOT) for TinyCloud capability action URNs (TC-112). Truth lives with the enforcer (tinycloud-node). Codegen (scripts/gen-capabilities.mjs) emits a Rust module and a TypeScript module from this file; the checked-in artifacts are verified against this registry in CI. Every entry is grounded in actual node dispatch code, not SDK declarations.

capabilities.notes: Per-service wildcard. Matched node-side as admin-equivalent in admin checks (routes/mod.rs:2139, sql/database.rs:177, sql/parser.rs:25) and as write+admin in validate_sql. Live in the SDK root delegation grant (TinyCloudNode.ts ~2272). Modeled as implying every concrete active sql action so a wildcard grant authorizes any sql request via implication expansion.

Per-service wildcard. Matched node-side as admin-equivalent (routes/mod.rs:2140, duckdb/parser.rs:29) and as write+admin in validate_sql; recognized by preferred_database_ability (routes/mod.rs:2118). Live in the SDK root delegation grant (TinyCloudNode.ts ~2279). Implies every concrete active duckdb action.

Per-service wildcard. P2 flipped execute+deploy to active (the handlers ship); `list` stays reserved (no server-side listing handler, §12.1/C9), so the wildcard implies ONLY the two active concretes (gen-capabilities.mjs requires a wildcard to imply exactly the *active* concrete actions of its service). F9 (SDK): the standard session grant MUST enumerate compute/execute (+ compute/list once it ships) and NEVER compute/* -- the wildcard confers deploy, which standard sessions must not hold.
Creates the space: a space/host delegation inserts the space row in transact() (tinycloud-core/src/db.rs ~818, matched on Delegation events).

Invoked by the SDK against /invoke (SpaceService.ts create(); TinyCloud.ts lazy public-space create). The node authorizes it via the delegation chain in the invocation-verification path (not via accepted_actions), but there is NO dedicated side-effect handler — db.rs invocation match falls through (_ => {}). Real space creation happens through space/host. 409/404 the SDK sees come from the space-existence check (TxError::SpaceNotFound), not a create handler.

Invoked by the SDK (SpaceService.ts listOwnedSpaces). Authorized via the delegation chain; no dedicated node side-effect handler (db.rs invocation match falls through). Owned-space discovery is served via the capabilities/read + host layers, not a space/list handler.

Invoked by the SDK (SpaceService.ts getSpaceInfo; TinyCloud.ts probe-before-create). Authorized via the delegation chain; no dedicated node side-effect handler. A nonexistent space yields TxError::SpaceNotFound -> 404, which is exactly the SDK's existence probe.
